title: phaseolaria praetermissa
intro: Phaseolaria praetermissa, commonly known as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, is native to South America. This plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The therapeutic actions of Phaseolaria praetermissa include wound healing, antiseptic properties, and the treatment of various skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Phaseolaria praetermissa can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged consumption may also cause damage to the liver and kidneys, leading to elevated levels of liver enzymes and decreased kidney function. Additionally, improper use of this plant may trigger allergic reactions, resulting in symptoms like hives, itching, and respiratory distress. In severe cases, excessive ingestion can lead to cardiac arrhythmias and even coma.
